stream_samples.text: DST flipped over the weekend and Calendly is showing all my UK invitees a 1-hour-shifted slot. booking page says 9am their time, calendar invite says 10am.
Connected my Outlook AND Google calendars and Calendly is double-booking me into Outlook events that are marked private. conflict detection only reads Google.
[#SCHED-4820 P1] customer: round-robin team of 8 SDRs assigning 71% of inbounds to two reps after the assignment-fairness migration on Apr 22
scheduling-core metrics: DST-week double-booking rate spiked 4.8x for users with multi-calendar connections. cross-tz invitees most affected.
Calendly Workflow 'send confirmation' fired three times for one rescheduled meeting today. invitee texted asking which time was real.
Group event with 6 capacity is letting in 8 people because the booking lock isn't holding under concurrent submits. third weekend in a row.
[#AVAIL-3210 P2] enterprise: Embeds on customer portal blocked by their CSP — frame-ancestors directive needs explicit calendly.com but our embed snippet doesn't document it
Slack integration: new-booking notifications going to #general instead of the channel I configured for that event type. routing rule ignored.
iOS app showing my availability in PT when my account is set to ET. invitees are getting confused booking pages all week.
support escalations: DST week saw 3.2x ticket volume vs baseline. 64% are timezone display or multi-calendar conflict. AE team flagged 4 enterprise pause-renewals.
round-robin assignment is supposed to be even but one rep on my team has 40 meetings this month and another has 6. fairness algorithm isn't.
is there a way to force Calendly to use a specific calendar's free/busy as primary and the others as read-only? for conflict detection only
Workflow trigger 'event rescheduled' is firing on cancellations too since the v2 trigger refactor — sending two follow-ups when invitees cancel
Calendly's new meeting polls feature actually replaced our doodle workflow cleanly today. small team meetings finally schedule themselves.
[scheduling-core] cross-calendar conflict check converts all events to invitee_tz before merge, dropping events where source_tz crosses DST boundary mid-window

clusters.title: DST timezone display + multi-calendar conflict misses
Group event capacity overbooking under concurrency
Embeds CSP blocking on enterprise portals

top_epic.title: Fix DST + multi-calendar conflict detection across timezones
top_epic.rationale: 2,040 signals across Zendesk, X and Slack converge on the same failure mode: during DST week, double-booking rate for users with multi-calendar connections spiked 4.8x. The cross-calendar conflict checker converts all events to invitee_tz before merging, silently dropping events whose source_tz crosses a DST boundary mid-window. 64% of DST-week tickets are timezone or conflict misses, and four enterprise accounts have paused renewal conversations.
top_epic.verdict: Trust-critical — a scheduling tool that double-books during DST loses the core reason customers buy it.
top_epic.recommendation: Move conflict detection to UTC-canonical merge before tz projection, ship per-calendar provenance in conflict logs, and add a DST-week canary that verifies double-book rate stays within baseline before promoting any scheduling-core change.
acceptance_criteria: Conflict merge happens in UTC before invitee_tz projection
Double-book rate during DST week within 1.2x baseline across all multi-calendar configs
Conflict log shows source calendar, source_tz, and projected_tz per event
DST-week canary blocks scheduling-core promotion on >1.5x double-book regression

evidence.quote: DST-week double-booking rate spiked 4.8x for users with multi-calendar connections.
Connected Outlook AND Google and Calendly is double-booking me into private Outlook events.
Conflict check converts all events to invitee_tz before merge, drops DST-boundary events.
